title: Title and Description Box Options for WorkForms
excerpt: Similar to the “title and description” option on Google Forms, I would like to add boxes of text to my workform. Being able to hyperlink and format the information (bold, italicize, bullet list, add photo, etc) in the te…

posts.author: xsarahwong (Sarah Wong)
posts.cookedHtml:
Similar to the “title and description” option on Google Forms, I would like to add boxes of text to my workform. Being able to hyperlink and format the information (bold, italicize, bullet list, add photo, etc) in the text boxes will also be a plus. This would allow us to add any additional information that may be needed to be fill out the questions below it. Since these would be text boxes, they don’t need to be included in the Main Table.

Allowing for multi-page forms or forms with sections can help organize the information into a cleaner-looking form.

posts.author: JLawrence (John Lawrence)
posts.cookedHtml: I agree a text box feature would help to elaborate on the nature or specifics of the question. This could also be added as an information icon which is otherwise hidden.

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @JLawrence

Nachliel from Easyapps here

Although there is no option of adding text boxes to forms, both Forms and WorkForms questions have the option of adding a description to them.

Regardless, if you are interested in creating styled forms that better match your brand and embed perfectly in you web page you can check out our monday.com app Easyform and our extensive documentation site.

You can set up a free demo here or contact us at support@easyappshq.com.

posts.author: HPETRIE (Hannah Petrie)
posts.cookedHtml:
Our team is also looking for this feature. Appreciate that it is available via an add on (Easyform) but I am surprised this is not built directly into the tool.

Monday.com team - do you have an update?
